column.name: MANDT
column.description: Client identifier in the SAP system (usually a three‑digit numeric code). It separates data belonging to different corporate groups; analysts filter on this column to isolate the business unit they are reporting on.

column.name: OBKNR
column.description: Object number that links the entry to a higher‑level business object (e.g., a purchase order, sales order, or production order). It is an alphanumeric key used for cross‑referencing related documents.

column.name: MBLNR
column.description: Material document number that records a goods movement (receipt, issue, transfer, etc.). This numeric key is unique per posting and is the primary reference for inventory audit trails.

column.name: MJAHR
column.description: Fiscal year of the material document (four‑digit year, e.g., 2024). Together with MBLNR it uniquely identifies a posting; analysts use it for period‑based reporting.

column.name: ZEILE
column.description: Line‑item number within the material document. It distinguishes multiple movements recorded under the same MBLNR; typical values are sequential integers starting at 1.

column.name: BLART
column.description: Document type (e.g., 'WE' for goods receipt, 'WA' for goods issue, 'WR' for transfer posting). This character field indicates the business process that generated the entry.

column.name: BWART
column.description: Movement type code (e.g., 101 for goods receipt, 201 for goods issue, 301 for transfer posting). It determines the inventory impact and the posting logic applied.

column.name: SHKZG
column.description: Debit/credit indicator – 'S' for a debit (stock increase) and 'H' for a credit (stock decrease). Analysts use this to calculate net inventory changes.

column.name: DATUM
column.description: Posting date of the material document in YYYYMMDD format. It is the date the inventory movement is considered effective for financial and logistical reporting.

column.name: UZEIT
column.description: Posting time (HHMMSS) that records the exact time of the transaction on the posting date. Useful for high‑frequency environments where multiple postings occur on the same day.

column.name: LETZNR
column.description: Sequential number of the last processed line for the given document; often used internally for error handling or re‑processing. Typically a small integer.

column.name: ANZSN
column.description: Quantity posted (numeric, with unit of measure defined elsewhere). Positive values represent stock additions, negative values represent removals, depending on SHKZG.

column.name: VORGANG
column.description: Transaction code or business process identifier (e.g., 'MIGO', 'MB1A'). It provides context on which SAP transaction created the entry.

column.name: LIEFERANT
column.description: Vendor (supplier) number associated with the movement, if applicable. Empty for internal transfers or customer‑driven issues.

column.name: KUNDE
column.description: Customer number linked to the movement, used mainly for sales‑related issues such as deliveries or returns.

column.name: WERK
column.description: Plant code where the material movement took place (four‑character alphanumeric). It defines the organizational location for inventory valuation.

column.name: LAGERORT
column.description: Storage location within the plant (e.g., '0010', 'A001'). It pinpoints the exact bin or area affected by the posting.

column.name: CHARGE
column.description: Batch or lot number of the material, if the material is batch‑managed. This field enables traceability of specific production lots.
